what: Analyze the Impact of Sensor Error Modelling on Navigation Performance
title: Analyze the Impact of Sensor Error Modelling on Navigation Performance
text: navigation R package allows analyze impact sensor error modeling performance integrated navigation (sensor fusion) based IMU, GPS (generally speaking, GNSS), barometer data. package allows one two major tasks: Sensor model evaluation: user shall provide reference trajectory, along navigation performance evaluated using different sensor error models. Perfect sensor data along reference trajectory generated, corrupted sensor error coming either simulation based error models provided user, directly user input (option added). Integrated navigation performed, whit separately provided error model used within Extended Kalman Filter (EKF). user can easily introduce GPS outage periods, growing number tools visualize summarize results. Integrated navigation (sensor fusion) natural -product first main application, integrated navigation also available users. Providing sensor data sensor error model used within navigation filter, user able perform integrated navigation using package also benefit subset visualization tools. Caution flat non-rotating Earth model assumed throughout package. consider major impact sensor model evaluation, main contributor match/mismatch additive sensor errors provided error models navigation filter. absolute navigation results though, long distances high speeds involved, simplifications start measurable impact results. Also, attitude parameterization done via Euler angles moment, bringing interinsic limitations, singularity pitch =±π/2=\pm \pi/2. limitation may resolved future using attitude parameterizations quaternions.

title: Compute Coverage — compute_coverage
code: # load data data("lemniscate_traj_ned") head(lemniscate_traj_ned) #>         t          x          y z         roll     pitch_sm       yaw #> [1,] 0.00 0.00000000 0.00000000 0 0.0000000000 0.000000e+00 0.7853979 #> [2,] 0.01 0.05235987 0.05235984 0 0.0001821107 8.255405e-05 0.7853971 #> [3,] 0.02 0.10471968 0.10471945 0 0.0003642249 1.650525e-04 0.7853946 #> [4,] 0.03 0.15707937 0.15707860 0 0.0005463461 2.474976e-04 0.7853905 #> [5,] 0.04 0.20943890 0.20943706 0 0.0007284778 3.298918e-04 0.7853847 #> [6,] 0.05 0.26179819 0.26179460 0 0.0009106235 4.122374e-04 0.7853773 traj <- make_trajectory(data = lemniscate_traj_ned, system = "ned") timing <- make_timing(   nav.start = 0, # time at which to begin filtering   nav.end = 20,   freq.imu = 100,   # frequency of the IMU, can be slower wrt trajectory frequency   freq.gps = 1, # GNSS frequency   freq.baro = 1,   # barometer frequency (to disable, put it very low, e.g. 1e-5)   gps.out.start = 10,   # to simulate a GNSS outage, set a time before nav.end   gps.out.end = 15 ) # create sensor for noise data generation snsr.mdl <- list() # this uses a model for noise data generation acc.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 5.989778e-05) +  AR1(phi = 9.982454e-01, sigma2 = 1.848297e-10) +   AR1(phi = 9.999121e-01, sigma2 = 2.435414e-11) +   AR1(phi = 9.999998e-01, sigma2 = 1.026718e-12) gyr.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 1.503793e-06) +  AR1(phi = 9.968999e-01, sigma2 = 2.428980e-11) +   AR1(phi = 9.999001e-01, sigma2 = 1.238142e-12) snsr.mdl$imu <- make_sensor(   name = "imu",   frequency = timing$freq.imu,   error_model1 = acc.mdl,   error_model2 = gyr.mdl ) # RTK-like GNSS gps.mdl.pos.hor <- WN(sigma2 = 0.025^2) gps.mdl.pos.ver <- WN(sigma2 = 0.05^2) gps.mdl.vel.hor <- WN(sigma2 = 0.01^2) gps.mdl.vel.ver <- WN(sigma2 = 0.02^2) snsr.mdl$gps <- make_sensor(   name = "gps",   frequency = timing$freq.gps,   error_model1 = gps.mdl.pos.hor,   error_model2 = gps.mdl.pos.ver,   error_model3 = gps.mdl.vel.hor,   error_model4 = gps.mdl.vel.ver ) # Barometer baro.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 0.5^2) snsr.mdl$baro <- make_sensor(   name = "baro",   frequency = timing$freq.baro,   error_model1 = baro.mdl ) # define sensor for Kalmna filter KF.mdl <- list() # make IMU sensor KF.mdl$imu <- make_sensor(   name = "imu",   frequency = timing$freq.imu,   error_model1 = acc.mdl,   error_model2 = gyr.mdl ) KF.mdl$gps <- snsr.mdl$gps KF.mdl$baro <- snsr.mdl$baro # perform navigation simulation num.runs <- 5 # number of Monte-Carlo simulations res <- navigation(   traj.ref = traj,   timing = timing,   snsr.mdl = snsr.mdl,   KF.mdl = KF.mdl,   num.runs = num.runs,   noProgressBar = TRUE,   PhiQ_method = "1",   # order of the Taylor expansion of the matrix exponential   # used to compute Phi and Q matrices   compute_PhiQ_each_n = 20,   # compute new Phi and Q matrices   # every n IMU steps (execution time optimization)   parallel.ncores = 1,   P_subsampling = timing$freq.imu ) # keep one covariance every second coverage <- compute_coverage(res, alpha = 0.7, step = 100, idx = 1:6) #>  #>  plot(coverage)

code: # load data data("lemniscate_traj_ned") head(lemniscate_traj_ned) #>         t          x          y z         roll     pitch_sm       yaw #> [1,] 0.00 0.00000000 0.00000000 0 0.0000000000 0.000000e+00 0.7853979 #> [2,] 0.01 0.05235987 0.05235984 0 0.0001821107 8.255405e-05 0.7853971 #> [3,] 0.02 0.10471968 0.10471945 0 0.0003642249 1.650525e-04 0.7853946 #> [4,] 0.03 0.15707937 0.15707860 0 0.0005463461 2.474976e-04 0.7853905 #> [5,] 0.04 0.20943890 0.20943706 0 0.0007284778 3.298918e-04 0.7853847 #> [6,] 0.05 0.26179819 0.26179460 0 0.0009106235 4.122374e-04 0.7853773 traj <- make_trajectory(data = lemniscate_traj_ned, system = "ned") timing <- make_timing(   nav.start = 0,   # time at which to begin filtering   nav.end = 20,   freq.imu = 100,   # frequency of the IMU, can be slower wrt trajectory frequency   freq.gps = 1, # GNSS frequency   freq.baro = 1,   # barometer frequency (to disable, put it very low, e.g. 1e-5)   gps.out.start = 10,   # to simulate a GNSS outage, set a time before nav.end   gps.out.end = 15 ) # create sensor for noise data generation snsr.mdl <- list() # this uses a model for noise data generation acc.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 5.989778e-05) +   AR1(phi = 9.982454e-01, sigma2 = 1.848297e-10) +   AR1(phi = 9.999121e-01, sigma2 = 2.435414e-11) +   AR1(phi = 9.999998e-01, sigma2 = 1.026718e-12) gyr.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 1.503793e-06) +   AR1(phi = 9.968999e-01, sigma2 = 2.428980e-11) +   AR1(phi = 9.999001e-01, sigma2 = 1.238142e-12) snsr.mdl$imu <- make_sensor(   name = "imu",   frequency = timing$freq.imu,   error_model1 = acc.mdl,   error_model2 = gyr.mdl ) # RTK-like GNSS gps.mdl.pos.hor <- WN(sigma2 = 0.025^2) gps.mdl.pos.ver <- WN(sigma2 = 0.05^2) gps.mdl.vel.hor <- WN(sigma2 = 0.01^2) gps.mdl.vel.ver <- WN(sigma2 = 0.02^2) snsr.mdl$gps <- make_sensor(   name = "gps",   frequency = timing$freq.gps,   error_model1 = gps.mdl.pos.hor,   error_model2 = gps.mdl.pos.ver,   error_model3 = gps.mdl.vel.hor,   error_model4 = gps.mdl.vel.ver ) # Barometer baro.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 0.5^2) snsr.mdl$baro <- make_sensor(   name = "baro",   frequency = timing$freq.baro,   error_model1 = baro.mdl ) # define sensor for Kalman filter KF.mdl <- list() # make IMU sensor KF.mdl$imu <- make_sensor(   name = "imu",   frequency = timing$freq.imu,   error_model1 = acc.mdl,   error_model2 = gyr.mdl ) KF.mdl$gps <- snsr.mdl$gps KF.mdl$baro <- snsr.mdl$baro # perform navigation simulation num.runs <- 5 # number of Monte-Carlo simulations res <- navigation(   traj.ref = traj,   timing = timing,   snsr.mdl = snsr.mdl,   KF.mdl = KF.mdl,   num.runs = num.runs,   noProgressBar = TRUE,   PhiQ_method = "1",   # order of the Taylor expansion of the matrix exponential   # used to compute Phi and Q matrices   compute_PhiQ_each_n = 10,   # compute new Phi and Q matrices every n IMU steps (execution time optimization)   parallel.ncores = 1,   P_subsampling = timing$freq.imu ) # keep one covariance every second   oe <- compute_mean_orientation_err(res, step = 25) plot(oe)

time at which to begin filtering   nav.end = 30,   freq.imu = 100,   # frequency of the IMU, can be slower wrt trajectory frequency   freq.gps = 1,   # GNSS frequency   freq.baro = 1,   # barometer frequency    # (to disable, put it very low, e.g. 1e-5)   gps.out.start = 20,    # to simulate a GNSS outage, set a time before nav.end   gps.out.end = 25 ) # create sensor for noise data generation snsr.mdl <- list() # this uses a model for noise data generation acc.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 5.989778e-05) +  AR1(phi = 9.982454e-01, sigma2 = 1.848297e-10) +   AR1(phi = 9.999121e-01, sigma2 = 2.435414e-11) +    AR1(phi = 9.999998e-01, sigma2 = 1.026718e-12) gyr.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 1.503793e-06) +  AR1(phi = 9.968999e-01, sigma2 = 2.428980e-11) +   AR1(phi = 9.999001e-01, sigma2 = 1.238142e-12) snsr.mdl$imu <- make_sensor(name = "imu",  frequency = timing$freq.imu,  error_model1 = acc.mdl,  error_model2 = gyr.mdl) # Barometer baro.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 0.5^2) snsr.mdl$baro <- make_sensor(   name = "baro",   frequency = timing$freq.baro,   error_model1 = baro.mdl ) # define sensor for Kalmna filter KF.mdl <- list() # make IMU sensor KF.mdl$imu <- make_sensor(   name = "imu",   frequency = timing$freq.imu,   error_model1 = acc.mdl, error_model2 = gyr.mdl ) KF.mdl$gps <- snsr.mdl$gps KF.mdl$baro <- snsr.mdl$baro # perform navigation simulation num.runs <- 5 # title: Construct a sensor object — make_sensor

title: Construct a sensor object — make_sensor
text: Davide Cucci, Lionel Voirol, Mehran Khaghani, Stéphane Guerrier

title: Construct a sensor object — make_sensor
code: # IMU: imu.freq <- 250 acc.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 1.535466e-04) + RW(gamma2 = 1.619511e-10) gyr.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 1.711080e-03) + RW(gamma2 = 1.532765e-13) imu.mdl <- make_sensor(   name = "imu",   frequency = imu.freq,   error_model1 = acc.mdl,   error_model2 = gyr.mdl )  # GPS: gps.freq <- 1 gps.mdl.pos.hor <- WN(sigma2 = 2^2) gps.mdl.pos.ver <- WN(sigma2 = 4^2) gps.mdl.vel.hor <- WN(sigma2 = 0.04^2) gps.mdl.vel.ver <- WN(sigma2 = 0.06^2) gps.mdl <- make_sensor(   name = "gps", frequency = gps.freq,   error_model1 = gps.mdl.pos.hor,   error_model2 = gps.mdl.pos.ver,   error_model3 = gps.mdl.vel.hor,   error_model4 = gps.mdl.vel.ver )  # Baro: baro.freq <- 1 baro.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 0.5^2) baro.mdl <- make_sensor(   name = "baro",   frequency = baro.freq,   error_model1 = baro.mdl )

title: Construct a trajectory object — make_trajectory
text: data multiple-column matrix. first column corresponds measurment time (seconds); columns 2, 3 4 corresponds positions (order lat, long alt (rad) ellipsoidal coord x_N, x_E x_D NED coord); columns 5, 6 7 (optional) corresponds attitude (order roll, pitch yaw); columns 8, 9 10 (optional) corresponds velocity along axes columns 2, 3 4. system string corresponding coordinate system (possible choices: ellipsoidal ned) considered. start_time string (optional) corresponding start time trajectory. name string (optional) corresponding name dataset. ... Additional arguments.

title: Construct a trajectory object — make_trajectory
text: object class trajectory.

title: Construct a trajectory object — make_trajectory
text: Davide Cucci, Lionel Voirol, Mehran Khaghani, Stéphane Guerrier

title: Runs
text: function performs two following main tasks, base provided input. reference trajectory (traj.ref) provided, generates sensor data (IMU, GPS, Baro) corrupted additive errors according snsr.mdl, performs navigation using KF.mdl sensor error model within Kalman filter evaluate particular model performs navigating.

title: Runs
code: navigation(   traj.ref,   timing,   snsr.mdl,   KF.mdl,   g = 9.8056,   num.runs = 1,   results.system = "ned",   x_o = NULL,   noProgressBar = FALSE,   IC = NULL,   imu_data = NULL,   gps_data = NULL,   baro_data = NULL,   input.seed = 0,   PhiQ_method = "exact",   P_subsampling = 1,   compute_PhiQ_each_n = 1,   parallel.ncores = detectCores(all.tests = FALSE, logical = TRUE),   tmpdir = tempdir() )

title: Runs
text: traj.ref trajectory object (see documentation make_trajectory), serving reference trajectory generating sensor data evaluating error navigation performed. position attitude data required/considered, velocity calculated position. timing timing object (see documentation make_timing) containing timing information start end navigation. snsr.mdl sensor object (see documentation make_sensor) containing additive sensor error model generate realistic sensor data. KF.mdl sensor object (see documentation make_sensor) containing additive sensor error model used within Kalman filter navigation. g Gravitational acceleration. num.runs Number times sensor data generation navigation performed (Monte-Carlo simulation). results.system coordinate system (ned/ellipsoidal) results reported (see documentation make_trajectory). x_o Origin fixed ned frame. noProgressBar bolean specifying progress bar. IC Initial conditions. See examples format. imu_data IMU data. See examples format. gps_data GPS data. See examples format. baro_data Baro data. See examples format. input.seed Seed random number generator. Actual seed computed input.seed * num.runs + run PhiQ_method String specify method compute Phi Q matrices, can "exact" order Taylor expansions use. P_subsampling (memory optimization) store one sample P matrix P_subsampling time instants. compute_PhiQ_each_n Specify interval IMU measurements computation PhiQ. parallel.ncores number cores used parallel Monte-Carlo runs. tmpdir store temporary navigation output. mapped filesystem lives RAM.

title: Runs
text: object navigation class containing reference trajectory, fused trajectory, sensor data, covariance matrix, time.

title: Runs
text: Davide Cucci, Lionel Voirol, Mehran Khaghani, Stéphane Guerrier

title: Runs
code: # load data data("lemniscate_traj_ned") head(lemniscate_traj_ned) #>         t          x          y z         roll     pitch_sm       yaw #> [1,] 0.00 0.00000000 0.00000000 0 0.0000000000 0.000000e+00 0.7853979 #> [2,] 0.01 0.05235987 0.05235984 0 0.0001821107 8.255405e-05 0.7853971 #> [3,] 0.02 0.10471968 0.10471945 0 0.0003642249 1.650525e-04 0.7853946 #> [4,] 0.03 0.15707937 0.15707860 0 0.0005463461 2.474976e-04 0.7853905 #> [5,] 0.04 0.20943890 0.20943706 0 0.0007284778 3.298918e-04 0.7853847 #> [6,] 0.05 0.26179819 0.26179460 0 0.0009106235 4.122374e-04 0.7853773 traj <- make_trajectory(data = lemniscate_traj_ned, system = "ned") timing <- make_timing(   nav.start = 0, # time at which to begin filtering   nav.end = 15,   freq.imu = 100, # frequency of the IMU, can be slower wrt trajectory frequency   freq.gps = 1, # GNSS frequency   freq.baro = 1, # barometer frequency (to disable, put it very low, e.g. 1e-5)   gps.out.start = 8, # to simulate a GNSS outage, set a time before nav.end   gps.out.end = 13 ) # create sensor for noise data generation snsr.mdl <- list() # this uses a model for noise data generation acc.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 5.989778e-05) +   AR1(phi = 9.982454e-01, sigma2 = 1.848297e-10) +   AR1(phi = 9.999121e-01, sigma2 = 2.435414e-11) +   AR1(phi = 9.999998e-01, sigma2 = 1.026718e-12) gyr.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 1.503793e-06) +   AR1(phi = 9.968999e-01, sigma2 = 2.428980e-11) +   AR1(phi = 9.999001e-01, sigma2 = 1.238142e-12) snsr.mdl$imu <- make_sensor(   name = "imu",   frequency = timing$freq.imu,   error_model1 = acc.mdl,   error_model2 = gyr.mdl ) # RTK-like GNSS gps.mdl.pos.hor <- WN(sigma2 = 0.025^2) gps.mdl.pos.ver <- WN(sigma2 = 0.05^2) gps.mdl.vel.hor <- WN(sigma2 = 0.01^2) gps.mdl.vel.ver <- WN(sigma2 = 0.02^2) snsr.mdl$gps <- make_sensor(   name = "gps",   frequency = timing$freq.gps,   error_model1 = gps.mdl.pos.hor,   error_model2 = gps.mdl.pos.ver,   error_model3 = gps.mdl.vel.hor,   error_model4 = gps.mdl.vel.ver ) # Barometer baro.mdl <- WN(sigma2 = 0.5^2) snsr.mdl$baro <- make_sensor(   name = "baro",   frequency = timing$freq.baro,   error_model1 = baro.mdl ) # define sensor for Kalmna filter KF.mdl <- list() # make IMU sensor KF.mdl$imu <- make_sensor(   name = "imu",   frequency = timing$freq.imu,   error_model1 = acc.mdl,   error_model2 = gyr.mdl ) KF.mdl$gps <- snsr.mdl$gps KF.mdl$baro <- snsr.mdl$baro # perform navigation simulation num.runs <- 5 # number of Monte-Carlo simulations res <- navigation(   traj.ref = traj,   timing = timing,   snsr.mdl = snsr.mdl,   KF.mdl = KF.mdl,   num.runs = num.runs,   noProgressBar = TRUE,   PhiQ_method = "1",   # order of the Taylor expansion of the matrix exponential   # used to compute Phi and Q matrices   compute_PhiQ_each_n = 10,   # compute new Phi and Q matrices every n IMU steps (execution time optimization)   parallel.ncores = 1,   P_subsampling = timing$freq.imu ) # keep one covariance every second

order of the Taylor expansion of the matrix exponential used to compute Phi and Q matrices   compute_PhiQ_each_n = 10,   # compute new Phi and Q matrices every n IMU steps (execution time optimization)   parallel.ncores = 1,   P_subsampling = timing$freq.imu )  nees <- compute_nees(res, idx = 1:6, step = 100) #>  #>  plot(nees)

make IMU sensor KF.mdl$imu <- make_sensor(   name = "imu",   frequency = timing$freq.imu,   error_model1 = acc.mdl,   error_model2 = gyr.mdl ) KF.mdl$gps <- snsr.mdl$gps KF.mdl$baro <- snsr.mdl$baro # perform navigation simulation num.runs <- 5 # number of Monte-Carlo simulations res <- navigation(   traj.ref = traj,   timing = timing,   snsr.mdl = snsr.mdl,   KF.mdl = KF.mdl,   num.runs = num.runs,   noProgressBar = TRUE,   PhiQ_method = "2",   # order of the Taylor expansion of the matrix exponential used to compute Phi and Q matrices   compute_PhiQ_each_n = 10,   # compute new Phi and Q matrices every n IMU steps (execution time optimization)   parallel.ncores = 1,   P_subsampling = timing$freq.imu ) plot_imu_err_with_cov(res, error=FALSE)
